questions.q: What was the purpose of building the Setu (bridge) in the Ramayana?
options: To cross the ocean and reach Lanka
To irrigate the forests of Kishkindha
To trap fish for the vanara army
To connect two banks of the river Sarayu
questions.explanation: The bridge was built across the ocean so Rama's army could cross over to Lanka to rescue Sita.

questions.q: Whose army built the great bridge to Lanka?
options: An army of human soldiers from Ayodhya
questions.explanation: The vanara host, allied with Rama, constructed the causeway across the sea.

questions.q: Across what body of water was the Setu built?
questions.explanation: The bridge spanned the ocean separating the mainland from the island of Lanka.

questions.q: What materials did the vanaras chiefly use to build the bridge?
questions.explanation: The vanaras hurled boulders, mountains, and uprooted trees into the sea to form the causeway.

questions.q: For whom was Rama leading the campaign that required the bridge?
questions.explanation: Rama crossed to Lanka to rescue his wife Sita, who had been abducted by Ravana.

questions.q: Who had abducted Sita, making the journey to Lanka necessary?
questions.explanation: Ravana, the rakshasa king of Lanka, carried Sita away, prompting Rama's expedition.

questions.q: According to popular tradition, why did some of the stones float on the water?
options: The name of Rama was written upon them
They were made of light pumice only
The gods enchanted them with wind
questions.explanation: Tradition holds that stones inscribed with Rama's name floated on the sea.

questions.q: What is the island kingdom that the bridge connected the mainland to?
questions.explanation: The bridge led across the sea to Lanka, Ravana's island stronghold.

questions.q: What kind of creatures made up the bridge-building host?
questions.explanation: The vanara host included monkeys and bears (such as Jambavan's troops) who carried the rocks.

questions.q: What was the bridge primarily meant to allow the army to do?
options: Walk across the sea on foot
questions.explanation: The causeway gave the army a path to march on foot across the water to Lanka.

questions.q: Which vanara architect is credited with engineering and directing the construction of the bridge?
questions.explanation: Nala, son of the divine craftsman Vishvakarma, supervised the building of the causeway.

questions.q: Before the bridge was built, what did Rama do to compel the ocean to grant passage?
options: He performed a penance (fast) on the shore
He built a boat from reeds
questions.explanation: Rama sat in penance on the shore for several days seeking the Ocean's help to cross.

questions.q: When the Ocean did not appear, how did Rama finally force a response?
options: He took up his bow and threatened to dry up the sea with his arrows
He cursed the fish to die
He commanded the wind to blow the water aside
He threw his crown into the waves
questions.explanation: An angered Rama raised his bow with fiery arrows, frightening the Ocean god into appearing.

questions.q: According to the Valmiki Ramayana, how did the Ocean god (Samudra) suggest the crossing be made?
options: By having Nala build a bridge, since the waters would bear it up
By sailing on a fleet of golden ships
By flying over on the bird Jatayu
questions.explanation: The Ocean advised that Nala, son of Vishvakarma, should build a bridge that the sea would support.

questions.q: How many days, by Valmiki's account, did the vanaras take to complete the bridge?
questions.explanation: Valmiki's Ramayana states the mighty vanaras completed the great bridge in five days.

questions.q: What was Nala's divine parentage that gave him the skill to build the bridge?
options: He was the son of Indra, king of the gods
He was the son of Vishvakarma, the celestial architect
He was the son of Agni, the fire god
He was the son of Surya, the sun god
questions.explanation: Nala was born of Vishvakarma, the divine architect, inheriting his craftsmanship.

questions.q: What did the vanaras uproot and carry from the land to cast into the sea?
questions.explanation: The vanaras tore up mountains and huge trees and hurled them into the ocean to form the bridge.

questions.q: Roughly how long was the bridge said to be in Valmiki's Ramayana?
options: Ten yojanas wide and a hundred yojanas long
Three yojanas wide and ten yojanas long
Fifty yojanas wide and fifty yojanas long
questions.explanation: Valmiki describes the bridge as ten yojanas in breadth and a hundred yojanas in length.

questions.q: Which key figure had recently joined Rama's side and was present as the army prepared to cross to Lanka?
questions.explanation: Vibhishana, Ravana's righteous brother, had defected to Rama before the crossing to Lanka.

questions.q: What role did the larger vanaras play while the architects positioned the stones?
options: They sang songs to calm the sea
They carried enormous rocks and trees to the bridge-head
They guarded the camp from a distance
They dug tunnels under the ocean floor
questions.explanation: The powerful vanaras hauled massive boulders and trees, which Nala arranged into the causeway.

questions.q: In which kanda (book) of the Valmiki Ramayana does the building of the bridge occur?
questions.explanation: The Setu Bandha episode appears in the Yuddha Kanda, the book describing the war against Ravana.

questions.q: After the bridge was finished, what did Rama's army do?
options: They crossed over it to reach Lanka
They waited for a fleet of ships
questions.explanation: Once the bridge was complete, the vast vanara army marched across it to Lanka to begin the war.

questions.q: What is the name of the Ocean god whom Rama propitiated for safe passage?
questions.explanation: The personified Ocean, called Samudra or Sagara, appeared before Rama and advised the bridge-building.

questions.q: When Rama drew his bow against the sea, where did the Ocean ask him to release the powerful arrow that had been invoked, so it would not be wasted?
options: Upon a region of robbers in the north (Drumakulya)
questions.explanation: The Ocean asked Rama to loose the arrow on the Drumakulya region in the north, inhabited by wicked dwellers.

questions.q: On which day of construction, per Valmiki, did the vanaras complete the greatest span, working with frenzied speed?
options: On the first day they built fourteen yojanas
On the third day twenty-one yojanas
Each successive day they extended it, finishing on the fifth
They built it all in a single hour
questions.explanation: Valmiki recounts the bridge growing over successive days, the vanaras completing the hundred-yojana span on the fifth day.

questions.q: Which sea or strait is traditionally identified with the location of Rama's bridge?
options: The Bay of Bengal near Kolkata
The strait between Rameswaram (India) and Mannar (Sri Lanka)
questions.explanation: The bridge is traditionally placed at the strait between Rameswaram in India and Mannar in Sri Lanka.

questions.q: Which divine craftsman, father of Nala, is the source of the bridge-builder's hereditary skill?
questions.explanation: Nala inherited his building genius from his father Vishvakarma, the architect of the gods.

questions.q: In Valmiki's account, what feature helped the vanaras keep the long bridge straight and aligned as they built?
options: They used a measuring cord and rods to keep it true
They followed a path of stars only
They let the current shape it randomly
questions.explanation: The vanaras used cords and measuring rods so the great causeway was built straight and even.

questions.q: What name is commonly given to the bridge in later tradition, honoring its builder and its purpose?
questions.explanation: The causeway is known as Nala Setu (after its architect) and as Rama Setu (after Rama).

questions.q: According to the narrative, how did the vanaras manage to make the heavy boulders and mountains stay in place to form a stable path?
options: They froze them with ice from the Himalayas
They bound the stones together and Nala arranged them so they held, the sea bearing them up as promised
They melted the rocks into a single sheet
They tied the bridge to the moon
questions.explanation: Nala arranged and bound the rocks into a firm causeway, and the Ocean, as promised, bore up the bridge.
